article:
In brief: We're in for a hot and relatively dry week ahead in Abuja, with temperatures soaring into the upper 30s and low 40s, especially by Wednesday. While there's a chance of rain on a few days, it's not looking like anything too significant will impact the city. So, if you have outdoor plans, make sure to stay hydrated and take breaks in the shade, especially if you're planning a visit to the National Assembly Complex or a stroll around the Millennium Park.

As we take a look at the current conditions, it's clear that the heat is already on. With a temperature of 34°C and a feels-like temperature of 39°C, it's essential to take the necessary precautions to stay cool. The humidity is at 52%, which is relatively moderate, but the UV index is at 8, which means it's crucial to wear sunscreen and protective clothing if you're planning to spend time outdoors. The wind is light, at just 4 km/h, which won't provide much relief from the heat.

Looking at the 7-day forecast, it's evident that we're in for a bit of a rollercoaster ride. [MAP: 7-day forecast map showing temperature and precipitation trends] We'll see a mix of partly cloudy and sunny skies, with temperatures fluctuating between the mid-30s and low 40s. Let's break down the forecast day by day to get a better understanding of what to expect.

### 2026-04-07
Today, we're expecting a high of 34°C and a low of 26°C, with a 40% chance of rain. While the rain chance is moderate, it's unlikely that we'll see any significant precipitation. The rain, if it does come, will likely be in the form of scattered thunderstorms, which could bring some brief relief from the heat. If you're planning to visit the Abuja National Mosque or the Nigerian National Museum, make sure to check the skies before heading out.

### 2026-04-08
Tuesday is looking similar to Monday, with a high of 33°C and a low of 25°C. The rain chance is slightly lower, at 35%, but we're still expecting partly cloudy skies. [MAP: satellite image showing cloud cover over Abuja] The wind will continue to be light, which means that any rain that does fall will be slow to clear. This could lead to some patchy fog in the morning, especially in low-lying areas around the city.

### 2026-04-09
Wednesday is when things start to heat up, with a high of 35°C and a low of 25°C. The rain chance increases to 50%, which is the highest we'll see all week. However, it's still unlikely that we'll see any significant precipitation. Instead, we're expecting partly cloudy skies with a chance of scattered thunderstorms. If you're planning to head to the Jabi Lake Mall or the Dala Hills, make sure to pack your sunscreen and stay hydrated.

### 2026-04-10
By Thursday, the heat will be on in full force, with a high of 39°C and a low of 25°C. The rain chance drops to just 3%, which means we're expecting mostly sunny skies. [MAP: temperature map showing the heat dome over Abuja] This will be one of the hottest days of the week, so make sure to take the necessary precautions to stay cool. If you're planning to attend an outdoor event, such as a game at the Abuja Stadium, make sure to bring plenty of water and take breaks in the shade.

### 2026-04-11
Friday will be slightly cooler, with a high of 36°C and a low of 26°C. The rain chance remains low, at just 5%, and we're expecting mostly sunny skies. [MAP: wind map showing the light breeze over Abuja] The wind will continue to be light, which means that any heat will be slow to dissipate. This will be a great day to get out and enjoy the city, whether you're visiting the National Arboretum or taking a stroll along the Shehu Shagari Way.

### 2026-04-12
The weekend is looking hot and dry, with a high of 37°C and a low of 26°C on Saturday. The rain chance is just 3%, and we're expecting mostly sunny skies. [MAP: satellite image showing the clear skies over Abuja] This will be a great day to get outdoors and enjoy the city, whether you're visiting the Abuja Zoo or taking a hike in the surrounding hills.

### 2026-04-13
By Sunday, we're expecting a bit of a change, with a high of 37°C and a low of 26°C. The rain chance increases to 18%, which is still relatively low, but it's something to keep an eye on. [MAP: forecast model showing the chance of precipitation on Sunday] We're expecting partly cloudy skies, with a chance of scattered thunderstorms. This could bring some relief from the heat, but it's still unlikely that we'll see any significant precipitation.

Overall, it's going to be a hot and dry week in Abuja, with temperatures soaring into the upper 30s and low 40s. While there's a chance of rain on a few days, it's not looking like anything too significant will impact the city. So, make sure to stay hydrated, wear sunscreen, and take breaks in the shade, especially if you're planning to spend time outdoors. We'll be keeping an eye on the forecast and will let you know if anything changes.
